description_long: This object is a preserved crab specimen, displaying a robust, dark brown carapace with a slightly rough texture. The crab features two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae), with one noticeably larger and lighter in color, transitioning from brown to a pale orange at the tips. Its body is supported by eight segmented walking legs, each jointed and tapering to a point, with a chitinous, slightly glossy surface. The carapace is broad and shield-like, with subtle ridges and a matte finish, while the underside is not visible. The eyes are small and positioned at the front of the carapace, likely on short stalks, though fine details are difficult to discern. The overall coloration ranges from dark brown to tan, with the claws exhibiting a gradient from brown to orange and black at the tips.
description: A preserved crab with a dark brown, rough carapace and two large, asymmetrical claws, one of which is pale orange. The crab has eight jointed legs and a matte, chitinous surface.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the broad carapace, both claws extended forward, and all eight legs splayed outward.
description_view_1: Left oblique view highlighting the larger, pale orange claw and the segmentation of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Rear view with the carapace's back edge visible, both claws partially retracted, and the legs extending backward.
description_view_3: Right oblique view emphasizing the smaller, darker claw and the curvature of the legs on this side.
